channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: 'Time was speeding up, slowing down, or even stopping': Physicist demonstrates a key theory of time by building a 'mini-universe' in his lab - Live Science
item.description: 'Time was speeding up, slowing down, or even stopping': Physicist demonstrates a key theory of time by building a 'mini-universe' in his lab  Live Science

item.title: A Jupiter-sized planet outlived its dying star, and NASA’s Webb has now read its atmosphere — the first ever detected on a world orbiting a white dwarf - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A Jupiter-sized planet outlived its dying star, and NASA’s Webb has now read its atmosphere — the first ever detected on a world orbiting a white dwarf  Space Daily

  3. Earth may survive the sun's death after all, new study suggests  Space
  4. Aerosols and hydrocarbons in the atmosphere of a white dwarf planet  Nature
  5. ‘Bizarre’ planet orbiting dead star may preview fate of our solar system  CNN

item.title: 50 Years Later, NASA's Voyager Probe Is Reaching A Big Milestone For Space Travel - Yahoo
item.description:
1. 50 Years Later, NASA's Voyager Probe Is Reaching A Big Milestone For Space Travel  Yahoo
  2. The Voyager 1 probe, launched in 1977, is now so far from Earth that a radio signal travelling at the speed of light takes over 22 hours to reach it, and it is still sending data back from interstellar space on a power source the size of a car battery  Space Daily
  3. NASA Has Not Launched Anything Faster Than Voyager in 48 Years. Here Is the Real Menu of Machines That Could Finally Beat It  19FortyFive
  4. In 2024, engineers rescued Voyager 1 after it spent months transmitting gibberish, remotely rewriting and relocating pieces of 46-year-old code around a failed memory chip from more than 24 billion kilometres away.  Space Daily
  5. NASA’s Voyager 1 left Earth in 1977; now its signal takes over 22 hours to reach us and it is still sending data  The Times of India

item.title: Scientists discover an Earth-like planet right next door - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Scientists discover an Earth-like planet right next door  Yahoo
  2. Astronomers discover a potentially habitable planet just 25 light-years away. 'This one is exciting'  Space
  3. UC Irvine astronomers discover a new Earth-like exoplanet  UC Irvine News
  4. Nearby super-Earth GJ 3378b may be a good candidate for life  EarthSky
  5. Scientists initially weighed the planet GJ 3378b at 5.3 Earth masses, a number that made it a likely gas world with crushing pressure — but 137 fresh telescope measurements revised that down to 2.3, nudging it across the boundary into rocky, potentially life-b  Space Daily

item.title: Asteroid 16 Psyche, a metal-rich world roughly 225 kilometres wide orbiting between Mars and Jupiter, may contain extraordinary amounts of iron and nickel — enough that back-of-the-envelope estimates have put its theoretical metal value at around $10,000 - Space Daily

item.title: Gold isn’t rare on Earth — it’s buried 3,000 kilometres too deep to reach. In 2025, scientists found that more than 99.999% of the planet’s precious metals are locked in the core, while Hawaiian lava carries traces suggesting some of that core material is slowly l - Space Daily
item.description: Gold isn’t rare on Earth — it’s buried 3,000 kilometres too deep to reach. In 2025, scientists found that more than 99.999% of the planet’s precious metals are locked in the core, while Hawaiian lava carries traces suggesting some of that core material is slowly l  Space Daily

item.title: For 21 years, Voyager 1’s primary roll thrusters were considered dead. Then in 2025, NASA tried to wake them up from 15 billion miles away — knowing that if they fired cold, they could explode, but if they stayed dead, the spacecraft might one day lose its aim - Space Daily
item.description: For 21 years, Voyager 1’s primary roll thrusters were considered dead. Then in 2025, NASA tried to wake them up from 15 billion miles away — knowing that if they fired cold, they could explode, but if they stayed dead, the spacecraft might one day lose its aim  Space Daily

item.title: We tend to think of Jupiter as permanently giant, but a 2025 study found it used to be twice its current size, with a magnetic field 50 times more powerful, and it is still quietly shrinking by about 2 centimetres every year - Space Daily

item.title: Save Hubble: the race to preserve the space telescope kicks off - Nature
